pattern: Enumerate every possible sequential-digit number by sliding a fixed-length window along the digit string '123456789', then collect those falling in the given range; the total count is tiny so brute generation is fast.
hints: There are only 36 sequential-digit numbers in existence (for lengths 2 through 9); generate them all rather than searching.
For a given length L, the sequential numbers are the substrings of '123456789' of length L interpreted as integers.
Generate all such numbers in increasing order by iterating lengths from 2 to 9, and for each length slide a window across '123456789', then keep only those inside [low, high].

edge_inputs.description: low equals high and that value is a sequential-digit number
range includes the largest sequential-digit number 123456789
range where no sequential-digit number exists
very tight range around a boundary where length changes
smallest possible range at the low boundary

stress.description: random low/high pairs across the full constraint
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    a = rng.randint(10, size)
    b = rng.randint(a, size)
    return [a, b]
